summary: End-to-end testing of AbsensiPro backend (31 pytest cases) and frontend (Playwright). All backend tests pass (auth, RBAC for monitor/manage, QR check-in geofence success/failure, face check-in without enrollment, check-out, duplicate check-in, dashboard stats shape, employees CRUD, offices). Frontend UI fully functional: owner login, dashboard, sidebar nav, monitoring, employees create+modal, offices create+modal, check-in panels render, logout, staff-role sidebar restrictions all verified.
action_items: Spec used data-testid 'emp-save' / 'emp-delete-{id}' but actual app uses 'save-employee-btn' / 'delete-{id}'. Same for offices ('save-office-btn'). Either update the spec or rename testids — not a bug, just a doc/spec mismatch.

checkin page exposes 'start-camera' and 'start-qr' testids only when face is enrolled AND the user is on the relevant tab. They are currently conditional renders. If you want them always discoverable for automation, render placeholders.
Offices page renders QR as canvas (qrcodejs) instead of ; if any external code expects , adjust. Visually verified working in owner screenshot.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~645 LOC and crosses the 700-line guideline — fine for now but plan to split into auth/attendance/employees/offices modules if it grows further.
FastAPI @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') is deprecated; consider lifespan context manager.
CORS allow_origins reads from env but uses '*' fallback while allow_credentials=False — OK since cookies not used (Bearer token in localStorage).
seed() rewrites /app/memory/test_credentials.md on every startup — fine, but unconditional file write to /app/memory could surprise other tooling.
validate_geofence is called BEFORE existing check-in check would block; consider reordering but not a bug.
Login module on frontend handles demo-account click but there can be a small race if click happens too quickly after page load (mitigated with wait_for_timeout).
test_credentials: owner@company.com / direksi@company.com / manager@company.com / dewi@company.com — all password123
seed_data_creation: Tests create employees with TEST_ prefix and clean up after themselves. UI test created one TEST_ui_<hash>@e.com employee and one TEST_Branch_<hash> office that are still in DB — safe to leave (TEST_ prefix).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Use BASE = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL + /api. Demo user passwords are reset by seed() on startup. Face check-in cannot be fully UI-automated (needs camera). Real testids for save: save-employee-btn / save-office-btn (NOT emp-save). Row actions: edit-{id} / delete-{id}. Login flow: fill email+password then click login-submit (wait 300ms after fill due to React state update). The 'demo-{role}' buttons sometimes race with form mount — using direct fill is more reliable.
rca of the issue: n/a — no functional issues detected; only spec-vs-impl naming differences for some testids.
